title: Feature List Display
content:
The application displays a comprehensive checklist of Firebase Studio features that were used to develop this page, yes, it might sound weird but I wanted to try the features that firebase studio has to offer without itroducing a lot of complexity from other products

In the left panel you'll find a list of these feature and how they were implemented in this webpage.

title: Prototype the prototype using the prototyper
content:
To deploy the front end of this app I took advantage of the prototyper provided by firebase studio which can create almost the entire application for you! just have to be very specific about the features that you want implemented in it

In this case the main prompt that I used was:

 'I want to create an app that features a list of all the features of firebase studio that can be used/implemented; it will be side-left panel that contains a checklist of said features, when you click on each one of these you will show information on how these features were implemented in the development of said app on an easy way.

I want all of this be taken from a json file that allows me to add information that will be loaded into the page, I want to be able to add images in the format of [img/path.jpg] inside of the json, then when the information is loaded into the page it will replace the [img/path.jpg] with the actual image'

 And as you can see taht's exactly what I got with the exception of another prompt that I used to add the dark mode and the left panel wasn't showing up correctly on a mobile device width and it is the following: 

'can you add a dark mode, and an option to display the left panel menu when the width of the screen is smaller?'

 which if I'm being honest, I could've placed everything in the first prompt, but I think it is good to check the results that you get from the prompt and work from there cause most likely it wont be perfect the first time.

title: Google Translate between you and the APIs
content:
The MCP server will interpret the actions you want to perform while talking to gemini and will make calls to the correspondant API if the action falls under certain parameters, 

 I just used the documentation https://firebase.google.com/docs/studio/mcp-servers#config-cli and it's pretty much plug and play, you even get the option for adding the firebase mcp server from a button once you create the mcp.json file in the .idx folder it looks something like this 

[adding the mcp server to firebase]

 and it add the json info required automatically

 After that's done you can reload everything and even tho it takes a little bit to start you'll see a tool icon on the gemini and if you click it it will display all the 'tools' that the mcp has access to

[mcp server tools]

i.e. if you tell gemini that you want to log in in firebase, it will make the API call that let's you log in in firebase and will ask you if you want to use the tool or not

[tool being executed]

 the tools provided by this kind of mcp server can be found https://firebase.google.com/docs/ai-assistance/mcp-server#tools

title: App Hosting (not Hosting)
content:
To deploy this app and to make it possible to reach you I used the documentation https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-hosting/get-started#create-app-hosting-backend It made it super easy to create a backend on which I was able to host this application 

 once the command firebase apphosting:backends:create --project [project-id-on-blaze-plan] was ran it pretty much gives you all the options that you need to setup, and even makes a conection with github actions to perform a deploy avery time a commit is merged into the main branch, it compiles everything and if it does without any errors App Hosting re-deploys the app, it's pretty handy tbh cause in the 'rollouts' tab of App Hosting it shows you the deployments that have been performed, the build id from cloud build and the commit from which said deployment was performed 

[img-comman firebase apphosting create]

title: ??? what is lighthouse?
content:
well lighthouse is a tool that you can use rightaway in your web preview of firebase studio, you just follow this https://firebase.google.com/docs/studio/debug#chrome-lighthouse and you wait a little bit, it gives a lot of useful information on how to optimize your application, it gives you rating on overall performance, Accessibility, Best practices, and SEO 

[img overall score on lighthouse]

title: You'll never be safe from annoying bugs
content: The first problem I encountered was with the github repo, although it was all set from my previous deployment (which didin't had any issues), I tried performing this same task from a new duplicated workspace, and all I had to do to set it up was to run the command 'git remote add origin https://github.com/github-name/github-repo' to link the new workspace with my old repository, and although it is not optimal, I did had to use the '-f' flag with the git push command to force the commit to happen

title: Where did my page went?
content: Here we are going to tackle the easiest 404 error that you may find, and how to spot it, nextJS uses folders, and page.tsx files to create the routes that the application will use, the 'home' page would be under src > app > page.tsx which is reacheable by just accessing the '/' route in your browser, now if you try to go to /login in this page you'll get a 404 cause I don't have it defined BUT if you go to /newPage you'll be able to see it since I have declared it under app > newPage > page.tsx

id: Errors, errors everywhere! The mighty 'Publish' button
title: I don't want to deploy to a new project, please help!
content: Yes! you can change which project will the publish button will deploy to, you just have to follow https://firebase.google.com/docs/studio/firebase-projects?hl=en&authuser=0#connect-manual and reload the workspace, it should update itself!
